questions: The fix means the editor pane will be smaller when it's the only visible pane (e.g., ~70% viewport instead of ~90%). Is that acceptable, or should we animate the height transition smoothly instead of pre-constraining?
Should the coexistence pre-computation only apply to the editor+generations pair, or also consider side panes (shots/tasks) reducing available viewport width?

The modified PanesContext.test.tsx in the working tree appears to have test expectations that don't match the current getEffectivePaneHeights code (e.g., the 'keeps generations at configured height' tier). Are these tests written for a new algorithm that's partially landed? Should I reconcile against the current code or the test expectations?
success_criteria: Toggling editor or generations pane visibility produces zero change in effectiveEditorPaneHeight and effectiveGenerationsPaneHeight
No visible snap/jump in LayoutMainContent padding when a pane toggles
Side-pane vertical centering (useBottomOffset) transitions smoothly without jumps
PaneControlTab slide animation uses correct target dimensions from the start
All existing PanesContext, LayoutMainContent, and useBottomOffset tests pass (updated as needed)
New test proves effective heights remain constant across visibility toggle
assumptions: The acceptable trade-off is that the editor pane is permanently constrained to its coexistence height (~70% viewport) even when the generations pane is hidden
The generations pane default height (PANE_CONFIG.dimensions.DEFAULT_HEIGHT = 350) is the correct base for coexistence computation
The working-tree modifications to PanesContext.tsx and its test file represent in-progress work that this plan should build on, not revert
Only the editor+generations vertical coexistence needs pre-computation; side panes (shots/tasks) don't have the same snap issue because they use width, not height
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
